summary: Frontend watchlist routes are configured as auth-gated and backed by reachable authenticated APIs, but honest browser validation of the live UI is blocked in this environment because agent-browser cannot launch due to missing libasound.so.2 runtime dependency.

evidence: frontend/src/router/index.ts beforeEach redirects unauthenticated requiresAuth routes to /login
GET /api/favorites/ returned 401 unauthenticated and 200 authenticated on backend http://localhost:8001
frontend/src/router/index.ts beforeEach redirects unauthenticated requiresAuth routes to /login
GET /api/watch/digests and /api/watch/rules returned 401 unauthenticated and 200 authenticated on backend http://localhost:8001
Authenticated API checks returned success=true for /api/watch/digests and /api/watch/rules
agent-browser install completed but launch failed with missing shared library libasound.so.2
browser validation could not capture screenshots or verify actual rendered redirects/components on localhost:3000

blockers.detail: agent-browser launch failed: chrome-headless-shell could not load libasound.so.2; agent-browser install --with-deps could not install system packages because sudo requires an interactive password.

frictions: Frontend routes /favorites and /watch return the generic SPA shell over raw HTTP, so route gating is only provable through router code and API responses without a functioning browser runtime.
Current authenticated datasets for favorites, watch digests, and watch rules are empty, so no future-milestone card behavior can be honestly claimed from user-visible evidence.
